solution.detail: Article 266 further says, No money out of this fund can be issued except in accordance with a parliamentary law. Thus, the authorization for the withdrawal of funds from the Consolidated Fund of India must come from the Parliament of India.
solution.extraEdge: Article 266 states that all receipts are credited to the Consolidated fund of India, and all payments are debited from it. All the legally authorised payments on behalf of the Government of India are made out of this fund

subject: Polity
stem: All revenues received by the Union Government by way of taxes and other receipts for the conduct of Government business are credited to the:

solution.verdict: Option C is correct: Consolidated Fund of India
solution.detail: The Consolidated Fund of India is the primary fund of the Union Government where all its revenues, including taxes, loans, and other receipts for the conduct of government business, are deposited. According to the Constitution of India, all government spending must be approved by Parliament and drawn from this fund. The accounts of Government are kept in three parts: 1. Consolidated Fund of India 2. Contingency Fund of India and 3. Public Account Different Funds With GoI CONSOLIDATED FUND OF INDIA (ARTICLE 266) PUBLIC ACCOUNT OF INDIA (Article 266) CONTINGENCY FUND OF INDIA (Article 267) It is a fund to which all receipts are credited and all payments are debited. All the legally authorised payments on behalf of the Government of India are made out of this fund. No money out of this fund can be issued except in accordance with a parliamentary law. All public money other than those which are credited to the CFI shall be credited here. Includes provident fund deposits, judicial deposits, savings bank deposits, departmental deposits, remittances and so on. Operated by executive action. Amounts determined by parliament by law are paid from time to time into this fund. Fund is placed at the disposal of the president, and he can make advances out of it to meet unforeseen expenditure. Held by the finance secretary on behalf of the president. It is operated by executive action. 12. SYSTEMS OF GOVERNMENT

stem: When the annual Union Budget is not passed by the Lok Sabha:
options: The Budget is modified and presented again
The Budget is referred to the Rajya Sabha for suggestions
The Union Finance Minister is asked to resign
The Prime Minister submits the resignation of Council of Ministers

solution.verdict: Option D is correct: The Prime Minister submits the resignation of Council of Ministers
solution.detail: If the Union Budget is not passed by the Lok Sabha, it indicates a lack of confidence in the government's financial proposals. Since the government is responsible for presenting and managing the budget, this failure typically results in a crisis of confidence. In such a scenario, the Prime Minister and the Council of Ministers are expected to resign, as the government's authority is effectively undermined.

subject: Polity
stem: What is the difference between “vote-on-account” and “interim budget”? 1. The provision of a “vote-on-account” is used by a regular Government, while an “interim budget” is a provision used by a caretaker Government. 2. A “vote-on-account” only deals with the expenditure in the Government’s budget, while an “interim budget” includes both expenditure and receipts. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?

solution.verdict: Option B is correct: 2 only
solution.detail: Statement 1 is incorrect: During the time of approaching elections, it is not practical to present a full budget, therefore the government presents an interim budget. Interim Budget can be presented by all governments whether incumbent or regular or caretaker. Statement 2 is correct: The Appropriation Bill becomes an Act after Presidential assent, authorizing withdrawals from the Consolidated Fund of India. Since its enactment takes time, the government cannot withdraw funds beyond March 31. To address this, the Lok Sabha grants a ‘Vote on Account’, allowing advance funds for essential expenses, usually for two months, covering one-sixth of the total estimated expenditure, until the Appropriation Bill is passed. A vote-on-account contains only the expenditure of the government’s budget while an Interim Budget is a complete set of accounts i.e. it includes both expenditure and receipts.

statements.text: During the time of approaching elections, it is not practical to present a full budget, therefore the government presents an interim budget. Interim Budget can be presented by all governments whether incumbent or regular or caretaker.

The Appropriation Bill becomes an Act after Presidential assent, authorizing withdrawals from the Consolidated Fund of India. Since its enactment takes time, the government cannot withdraw funds beyond March 31. To address this, the Lok Sabha grants a ‘Vote on Account’, allowing advance funds for essential expenses, usually for two months, covering one-sixth of the total estimated expenditure, until the Appropriation Bill is passed. A vote-on-account contains only the expenditure of the government’s budget while an Interim Budget is a complete set of accounts i.e. it includes both expenditure and receipts.

It facilitates the proper distribution of finances among the Public Sector Undertakings
It ensures transparency in financial administration

solution.verdict: Option D is correct: None of the statements
solution.detail: The Finance Commission of India is established under Article 280 of the Constitution. Its primary role is to make recommendations on the distribution of tax revenues between the Centre and the States and among the States themselves. Statement (a) is incorrect: Encouraging foreign capital inflow is not within the mandate of the Finance Commission. This is more relevant to policies of the Ministry of Finance or organizations like the RBI. Statement (b) is incorrect: The Finance Commission is not concerned with PSUs; its role is limited to tax revenue distribution between Centre and States and providing grants-in-aid. Statement (c) is incorrect: While the Finance Commission ensures equitable financial distribution, transparency in financial administration is not explicitly its function. This role is more aligned with bodies like the Comptroller and Auditor General (CAG). Thus, none of the given statements are correct in the context of the Finance Commission.

stem: At present, scientists can determine the arrangement or relative positions of genes of DNA sequences on a chromosome. How does this knowledge benefit us? 1. It is possible to know the pedigree of livestock. 2. It is possible to understand the causes of all human diseases. 3. It is possible to develop disease-resistant animal breeds. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?

solution.verdict: Option A is correct: 1 and 2 only
solution.detail: DNA Sequencing means determining the order of the four chemical building blocks - called “bases” - that make up the DNA molecule. This sequence informs scientists the kind of genetic information that is carried in a particular DNA segment. Statement 1 is correct: Pedigree analysis is the examination of a particular trait that is inherited from generation to generation. It helps in understanding the inheritance pattern of that trait and determining whether it is dominant or recessive. A thorough pedigree analysis using pedigree files received via DNA sequencing from the relevant breed organisations will certainly help in interpreting the pattern of inheritance of a livestock. Statement 2 is correct: Mapping genes allows researchers to identify genetic variations associated with diseases. This understanding facilitates the development of targeted therapies and personalized medicine approaches. Statement 3 is incorrect: Knowledge of gene positions enables the identification of genes responsible for disease resistance. This information can be utilized to breed or genetically engineer animals that are more resilient to specific diseases. However UPSC has not recognised it as completely true as if this application of DNA sequencing is still under development.

subject: Science and Tech
stem: Regular intake of fresh fruits and vegetables is recommended in the diet since they are a good source of antioxidants. How do antioxidants help a person maintain health and promote longevity?
options: They activate the enzymes necessary for vitamin synthesis in the body and help prevent vitamin deficiency
They prevent excessive oxidation of carbohydrates, fats and proteins in the body and help avoid unnecessary wastage of energy
They neutralize the free radicals produced in the body during metabolism
They activate certain genes in the cells of the body and help delay the ageing process

solution.verdict: Option C is correct: They neutralize the free radicals produced in the body during metabolism
solution.detail: Any substance that can inhibit the formation of unstable molecules known as free radicals, which harm DNA, cell membranes, and other components of cells, is referred to as an “antioxidant.” Free radicals grab electrons from other molecules in order to complete their electron complement, which causes those molecules to suffer damage. Regular consumption of fresh fruits and vegetables is recommended because they are rich sources of antioxidants. Antioxidants play a crucial role in maintaining health and promoting longevity by neutralizing free radicals produced during metabolism. Free radicals are unstable molecules that can damage cells, proteins, and DNA, contributing to ageing and various diseases. By donating electrons, antioxidants stabilize free radicals, thereby preventing cellular damage and supporting overall health. 3. DISEASES

subject: Science and Tech
stem: What is the difference between Bluetooth and Wi-Fi devices?
options: Bluetooth uses 2.4 GHz radio frequency band, whereas Wi-Fi can use 2.4 GHz or 5 GHz frequency band
Bluetooth is used for Wireless Local Area Networks (WLAN) only, whereas Wi-Fi is used for Wireless Wide Area Networks (WWAN) only

When information is transmitted between two devices using Bluetooth technology, the devices have to be in the line of sight of each other, but when Wi-Fi technology is used the devices need not be in the line of sight of each other

solution.verdict: Option A is correct: Bluetooth uses 2.4 GHz radio frequency band, whereas Wi-Fi can use 2.4 GHz or 5 GHz frequency band
solution.detail: Option (a) is correct: Bluetooth operates in the 2.4 GHz Industrial, Scientific, and Medical (ISM) band. Wi-Fi, depending on the standard, operates in the 2.4 GHz and/or 5 GHz bands. Option (b) is incorrect: Bluetooth is typically used for shortrange communication, forming Personal Area Networks (PANs), such as connecting peripherals like headphones or keyboards to a computer. Wi-Fi is used for Wireless Local Area Networks (WLANs), providing network connectivity within a local area like a home or office. Neither Bluetooth nor Wi-Fi is typically used for Wireless Wide Area Networks (WWANs), which cover broader areas and are usually associated with cellular networks. Option (c) is incorrect: Bluetooth does not require a direct line of sight between devices as it can transmit data through obstacles like walls or furniture, though the presence of obstacles can affect the range and quality of the connection. Wi-Fi also does not require line of sight and can cover greater distances than Bluetooth, with its signals capable of passing through walls and other obstructions, though signal strength may degrade with obstacles. Option (d) is incorrect: Since statement (b) is incorrect the fourth statement (“The statements (a) and (b) given above are correct in this context”) is also incorrect.

subject: Science and Tech
stem: What is a “Virtual Private Network”?
options: It is a private compute network of an organization where he remote users can transmit encrypted information through the server of the organization.
It is a computer network across a public internet that provides users access to their organization’s network while maintaining the security of the information transmitted.
It is a computer network in which users can access a shared pool of computing resources through a service provider

solution.verdict: Option B is correct: It is a computer network across a public internet that provides users access to their organization’s network while maintaining the security of the information transmitted.
solution.detail: A VPN is a technology that creates a secure, encrypted connection(computer network) over a public internet network (such as the internet) to allow remote users to access their organization’s private network securely. It ensures that data transmitted between the user and the organization’s network is protected from interception or unauthorized access. A VPN encrypts the data transmitted between the user’s device and the VPN server. This ensures that even if the data is intercepted it cannot be read by unauthorized parties. They allow users to bypass geo-restrictions and access content that may be blocked in their region (e.g., streaming services, websites, or social media platforms).

subject: Science and Tech
stem: An artificial satellite orbiting around the Earth does not fall down. This is so because the attraction of Earth_______
options: Does not exist at such distance
Is neutralized by the attraction of the moon
Provides the necessary speed for its steady motion
Provides the necessary acceleration for its motion

solution.verdict: Option D is correct: Provides the necessary acceleration for its motion
solution.detail: Artificial satellites are launched into orbit for a variety of purposes, including communications, navigation, Earth imaging, weather observation, and scientific study. In satellites, humans typically experience a sense of weightlessness because the net force acting on our body is practically zero. This is due to the satellite’s centrifugal force, which cancels out the size of the earth’s gravitational pull. On the other hand, the earth’s gravitational pull is required for the satellite to continuously change its direction, giving acceleration for the satellite’s direction-changing process.

subject: Science and Tech
stem: Which is the difference between asteroids and comets? 1. Asteroids are small rocky planetoids, while comets are formed of frozen gases held together by rocky and metallic material. 2. Asteroids are found mostly between the orbits of Jupiter and Mars, while comets are found mostly between Venus and Mercury. 3. Comets show a perceptible glowing tail, while asteroids do not. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?

solution.verdict: Option B is correct: 1 and 3 only
solution.detail: Statement 1 is correct: Asteroids are primarily composed of rock and metal and are remnants from the early solar system. In contrast, Comets are made of ice, dust, and rocky material and originate from the Kuiper Belt or Oort Cloud. Statement 2 is incorrect: Asteroids are predominantly located in the asteroid belt between Mars and Jupiter. Comets originate from distant regions of the solar system: short-period comets come from the Kuiper Belt beyond Neptune, and long-period comets originate from the distant Oort Cloud. They are not typically found between Venus and Mercury. Statement 3 is correct: As comets approach the Sun, the heat causes their icy components to sublimate, forming a glowing coma and often a visible tail that points away from the Sun due to solar wind. Asteroids, being rocky and lacking volatile materials, do not exhibit such features.

stem: Satellites used for telecommunication relay are kept in a geostationary orbit. A satellite is said to be in such an orbit when: 1. The orbit is geosynchronous. 2. The orbit is circular. 3. The orbit lies in the plane of the Earth’s equator. 4. The orbit is at an altitude of 22,236 km. Select the correct answer using the codes given below:

solution.verdict: Option A is correct: 1, 2 and 3 only
solution.detail: Statement 1 is correct: Geostationary Orbit is a type of geosynchronous orbit. The earth rotates in the same direction that satellites do. It lasts for roughly 24 hours and holds its place in relation to the earth. Statement 2 is correct: The orbit must be nearly circular, ensuring that the satellite’s distance from Earth remains constant throughout its revolution. An elliptical orbit would cause the satellite to move closer and farther from Earth, disrupting its stationary position. Statement 3 is correct: A Geostationary Orbit (GEO) is a special case of a Geosynchronous Orbit (GSO) where the satellite orbits exactly in the Earth’s equatorial plane (0° inclination). This ensures that the satellite appears fixed over a specific point on Earth. Statement 4 is incorrect: Geostationary orbit is at an altitude of around 35,786 km.

subject: Science and Tech
stem: The surface of a lake is frozen in severe winter, but the water at its bottom is still liquid. What is the reason?
options: Ice is a bad conductor of heat
Since the surface of the lake is at the same temperature as the air, no heat is lost
The density of water is maximum at 4°C

solution.verdict: Option C is correct: The density of water is maximum at 4°C
solution.detail: In severe winter, a lake’s surface freezes while the bottom remains liquid due to the anomalous density behavior of water. Unlike most substances, water reaches its maximum density at 4°C, meaning that as it cools, denser water sinks while colder, lighter water (below 4°C) stays at the surface. When temperatures drop below 0°C, the surface water freezes first, forming an insulating ice layer that prevents further heat loss from the deeper water, keeping it in a liquid state. This phenomenon protects aquatic life, as it prevents the entire lake from freezing solid

subject: Science and Tech
stem: Microbial fuel cells are considered a source of sustainable energy. Why? 1. They use living organisms as catalysts to generate electricity from certain substrates. 2. They use a variety of inorganic materials as substrates. 3. They can be installed in wastewater treatment plants to cleanse water and produce electricity. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?

solution.verdict: Option C is correct: 1 and 3 only
solution.detail: Microbial fuel cells (MFCs) are a bioelectrochemical technique, proposed to generate power using electrons from biological reactions in microorganisms. Statement 1 is correct: These devices primarily use bacteria as catalysts to break down organic and inorganic substances, generating electrical current. Statement 2 is incorrect: Microorganisms oxidise organic compounds, not inorganic substrates in the anode chamber, producing electrons and protons (reaction of anodes). Statement 3 is correct: An MFC typically consists of two electrodes: the anode and the cathode. At the anode, bacteria decompose organic matter in wastewater, producing electrons and protons. The electrons travel through an external circuit to the cathode, generating electricity, while the protons move from the anode to the cathode. At the cathode, the electrons and protons combine with an electron acceptor, such as oxygen, to form water. As a result, MFCs can be implemented in wastewater treatment facilities to purify water and generate electricity.

subject: Science and Tech
stem: The function of heavy water in a nuclear reactor is to
options: Slow down the speed of neutrons

solution.verdict: Option A is correct: Slow down the speed of neutrons
solution.detail: Moderator is used in a nuclear reactor for slowing down the neutrons produced during the fission reaction so that the chain reaction can be sustained. Due to its high moderating ratio and low neutron absorption cross section, heavy water makes a good moderator. Deuterium oxide, popularly known as heavy water (D2O), is a hydrogen isotope with a mass double that of regular hydrogen and oxygen. However, In nuclear power reactors, heavy water can also be utilized as a coolant.

subject: Science and Tech
stem: A company marketing food products advertises that its items do not contain trans fats. What does this campaign signify to the customers? 1. The food products are not made out of hydrogenated oils. 2. The food products are not made out of animal fats/oils. 3. The oils used are not likely to damage the cardiovascular health of the consumers. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?

solution.verdict: Option C is correct: 1 and 3 only
solution.detail: Trans fatty acids (TFA) or trans fats are a form of unsaturated fat and come in both natural and artificial forms. Trans fats are the most harmful type of fats which can cause an adverse effect on a human body than any other dietary constituent. Statement 1 is correct: Artificial trans fats are generated through an industrial process that adds hydrogen to liquid vegetable oils to form them more solid. That is hydrogen is made to react with the oil to produce fats that resemble pure ghee or butter. If items do not contain trans-fats, it means the food products are not made out of hydrogenated oils and animal fats/oils. Statement 2 is incorrect: This is incorrect because not all animal fats contain trans fats. While small amounts of naturally occurring trans fats are found in some animal products, the harmful trans fats that raise cardiovascular risks are primarily from industrial hydrogenation, not from natural animal fats. Statement 3 is correct: If items do not contain trans fats, it suggests that the oils used are less likely to harm the cardiovascular health of consumers, as oils associated with trans fats typically raise LDL (bad) cholesterol and lower HDL (good) cholesterol, contributing to plaque buildup in arteries and increasing the risk of heart attacks and strokes.

subject: Science and Tech
stem: What is the difference between a CFL and an LED lamp? 1. To produce light, a CFL uses mercury vapour and phosphor while an LED lamp uses semiconductor material. 2. The average lifespan of a CFL is much longer than that of an LED lamp. 3. A CFL is less energy-efficient as compared to an LED lamp. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?

solution.verdict: Option C is correct: 1 and 3 only
solution.detail: Statement 1 is correct: An LED is a type of semiconductor light source with two leads. It is a p-n junction diode, which when turned on emits light. A fluorescent lamp is a low-pressure mercury-vapour gas-discharge light. In a CFL, an electric current is driven through a tube containing argon and a small amount of mercury vapor. This generates invisible ultraviolet light that excites a fluorescent coating (called phosphor) on the inside of the tube, which then emits visible light. Statement 2 is incorrect: LED bulbs are much more efficient than CFL bulbs in terms of power consumption and life years. Statement 3 is correct: LED bulbs consume less power as compared to CFL. LED bulbs are expensive as compared to CFL bulbs. LED bulbs are much more efficient than CFL bulbs.

subject: Science and Tech
stem: Aspartame is an artificial sweetener sold in the market. It consists of amino acids and provides calories like other amino acids. Yet, it is used as a low-calorie sweetening agent in food items. What is the basis of this use?
options: Aspartame is as sweet as table sugar, but unlike table sugar, it is not readily oxidised in the human body due to lack of requisite enzymes.
When aspartame is used in food processing, the sweet taste remains, but it becomes resistant to oxidation.
Aspartame is as sweet as sugar, but after ingestion into the body, it is converted into metabolites that yield no calories.
Aspartame is several times sweeter than table sugar, hence food items made with small quantities of aspartame yield fewer calories on oxidation.

solution.verdict: Option D is correct: Aspartame is several times sweeter than table sugar, hence food items made with small quantities of aspartame yield fewer calories on oxidation.
solution.detail: Aspartame is a non-saccharide artificial sweetener approximately 200 times sweeter than sucrose, and is commonly used as a substitute of sugar in foods and beverages. The commercial names for it are NutraSweet, Equal, and Canderel. It is a methyl ester of the aspartic acid / phenylalanine dipeptide. Since Aspartame is several times sweeter than table sugar, hence food items made with small quantities of aspartame yield fewer calories on oxidation.

subject: Science and Tech
stem: Recently, “oilzapper” was in the news. What is it?
options: It is an eco-friendly technology for the remediation of oily sludge and oil spills
It is the latest technology developed for under-sea oil exploration
It is a genetically engineered high biofuel-yielding maize variety
It is the latest technology to control the accidentally caused flames from oil wells.

solution.verdict: Option A is correct: It is an eco-friendly technology for the remediation of oily sludge and oil spills
solution.detail: Oilzapper is an eco-friendly technology for oil sludges and oil spills remediation. Oilzapping is the new technique using bacteria to get rid of oil spills. It is a bio-remediation technique that involves the use of oil zapping bacteria. In essence, Oilzapper is a concoction of five distinct bacterial strains that have been immobilised and combined with a carrier substance (powdered corn cob). The Oilzapper consumes the hydrocarbon molecules found in crude oil and oily sludge, a dangerous hydrocarbon waste product produced by oil refineries, and transforms them into harmless CO2 and water. This is exactly what its witty name implies.

stem: A married couple adopted a male child. A few years later, twin boys were born to them. The blood group of the couple is AB positive and O negative. The blood group of the three sons is A positive, B positive, and O positive. The blood group of the adopted son is
options: Cannot be determined on the basis of the given data

solution.verdict: Option A is correct: O positive
solution.detail: The blood group of the couple is AB Positive and O Negative. So their biological sons will have either blood group A or B. Thus we can infer that the blood group of the adopted son will be O.

It is a type of digital optical disc storage that can hold any kind of digital data but is frequently used to store pictures, audio, and video in standard definition. Blu-ray Disc is a digital disc storage format similar to CD and DVD, that is designed to record and play a large amount of data with high-definition quality. Statement 3 is incorrect: The size of the Blu-ray disc is 120 millimetres in diameter with 1.2 millimetres thickness, which is the same as the size of a CD and DVD.

subject: Science and Tech
stem: When the bark of a tree is removed in a circular fashion all around near its base, it gradually dries up and dies because:
options: Water from soil cannot rise to aerial parts
Tree is infected by soil microbes
Roots do not receive oxygen for respiration

solution.verdict: Option B is correct: Roots are starved of energy
solution.detail: The tissue just beneath the bark called the phloem layer is in charge of moving food produced by photosynthesis from the leaves to the roots. This process is called girdling, and it is fatal to trees because it disrupts the transport of nutrients. Without this nourishment, the roots eventually perish and stop supplying the leaves with water and nutrients. Later, the leaves wither. Because its roots are starved of vitality, a tree that has had its bark removed in a circular pattern all around near its base gradually dries up and dies.

subject: Science and Tech
stem: Consider the following: 1. Photosynthesis 2. Respiration 3. Decay of organic matter 4. Volcanic action Which of the above add carbon dioxide to the carbon cycle on Earth?

solution.verdict: Option C is correct: 2, 3 and 4 only
solution.detail:
The carbon cycle is a natural process that involves the movement of carbon between the atmosphere, oceans, soil, rocks, and living organisms. Respiration, decay of organic matter, volcanic action, fossil fuel combustion, deforestation, ocean-atmosphere exchange adds carbon to the cycle.
- All living organisms (plants, animals, and microorganisms) respire and breaking down glucose and oxygen to release energy, CO₂, and water. This process adds carbon dioxide to the atmosphere.
- When dead plants, animals, and other organic materials decompose microbes break them down. During decomposition CO₂ and methane (CH₄) are released into the atmosphere.
- Volcanic eruptions release stored carbon dioxide from the Earth’s mantle into the atmosphere. This is a natural source of CO₂ emissions. During photosynthesis plants, algae, and some bacteria absorb CO₂ from the atmosphere and use sunlight to convert it into glucose and oxygen. This process removes carbon dioxide from the atmosphere rather than adding it.
